content:
# Pre-Rush Preparation Checklist

**Tool ID:** T087-01
**Block:** B087 (Pre-Rush Mise-en-Place)
**Domain:** Operations & Workflow (D05_operations_workflow)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Shift Lead
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. In professional kitchens they call it mise en place: everything in its place. If you are scrambling for milk at 08:15, you have already lost the rush.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Shift Lead working in Operations & Workflow.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Comprehensive station-by-station checklist covering every item that must be in position before the morning rush: bar supplies, till setup, food display, floor readiness, and support stock levels.

This tool supports **Pre-Rush Mise-en-Place** (B087) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Shift Lead (completing and verifying), opening team (executing)
**Time Required:** 10-15 minutes to verify. Prep work itself takes 30-45 minutes from 06:30.
**Frequency:** Every single day before the morning rush (complete by 07:15). Repeat before lunch rush (by 11:15) if applicable.

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (laminated, mounted near the pass or POS)
- Timer or clock visible from all stations
- Full staffing for opening shift

**Steps:**
1. Opening team begins prep tasks from 06:30 (or your opening time)
2. Shift Lead walks all stations at 07:15 using this checklist
3. Mark each item: READY or NOT READY
4. Any NOT READY items must be resolved BEFORE doors open or before 07:30
5. Brief the team: "We are rush-ready" or "I need [X] done in the next 10 minutes"

---

## Checklist

**Target: Complete by 07:15. All items READY before 07:30.**

### Station 1: Espresso Bar

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix Required |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 1.1 | Machine warmed up (minimum 20 min, pressure stable) | R | NR | |
| 1.2 | Grinder dialled in -- first 3 shots within recipe (18g > 36g / 25-30s) | R | NR | Current: _____g / _____s |
| 1.3 | Hopper full (enough beans for morning rush, approx 2kg) | R | NR | |
| 1.4 | Backup beans within reach (sealed bag, labelled) | R | NR | |
| 1.5 | Milk fridged and within reach: whole ___L, semi ___L, oat ___L, soy ___L | R | NR | |
| 1.6 | Milk jugs clean and at station (2 x small, 2 x large minimum) | R | NR | |
| 1.7 | Cups stacked and warm (espresso, small, medium, large) | R | NR | |
| 1.8 | Takeaway cups, lids, sleeves stocked (30+ of each size) | R | NR | |
| 1.9 | Scales zeroed and working, timer ready | R | NR | |
| 1.10 | Knock box empty, clean cloth at station, bin liner in place | R | NR | |
| 1.11 | Syrups stocked and pumps clean (vanilla, caramel, hazelnut, seasonal) | R | NR | |
| 1.12 | Chocolate powder, cinnamon, cocoa at station | R | NR | |

### Station 2: POS / Till Area

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix Required |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 2.1 | POS system on and logged in | R | NR | |
| 2.2 | Card reader charged and connected | R | NR | |
| 2.3 | Cash float counted and correct (EUR _____) | R | NR | |
| 2.4 | Receipt paper stocked | R | NR | |
| 2.5 | Loyalty cards / stamps available | R | NR | |
| 2.6 | Today's specials entered into POS (if applicable) | R | NR | |
| 2.7 | Takeaway bags and napkins stocked | R | NR | |

### Station 3: Food Display and Prep

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix Required |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 3.1 | Display case clean and lit | R | NR | |
| 3.2 | Pastries / baked goods displayed with labels and prices | R | NR | |
| 3.3 | Allergen labels on all displayed food items | R | NR | |
| 3.4 | Sandwich / food prep ingredients portioned and ready | R | NR | |
| 3.5 | Display fridge temperature checked: _____ deg C (target below 5 deg C) | R | NR | |
| 3.6 | Date labels on all prepped items checked (nothing expired) | R | NR | |
| 3.7 | Cutlery, plates, and serviettes stocked at service point | R | NR | |

### Station 4: Floor and Customer Area

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix Required |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 4.1 | All tables clean and reset (cleared, wiped, condiments in place) | R | NR | |
| 4.2 | Chairs aligned, floor swept (no crumbs from last night) | R | NR | |
| 4.3 | Customer toilets checked (clean, stocked, lights working) | R | NR | |
| 4.4 | Menu boards current (today's specials, any sold-out items removed) | R | NR | |
| 4.5 | Music on at appropriate volume | R | NR | |
| 4.6 | Lights on, heating/AC set, doors unlocked at opening time | R | NR | |
| 4.7 | A-board / outdoor signage placed (if applicable) | R | NR | |
| 4.8 | Water jug / self-service water station filled | R | NR | |

### Station 5: Support Stock (Behind the Scenes)

| # | Item | Ready | Not Ready | Fix Required |
|---|------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 5.1 | Backup milk in walk-in / back fridge (enough for full day) | R | NR | |
| 5.2 | Backup cups and lids accessible (not buried behind deliveries) | R | NR | |
| 5.3 | Cleaning supplies stocked (sanitiser spray, cloths, bin bags) | R | NR | |
| 5.4 | Dishwasher/glasswasher on, clean, and ready | R | NR | |

### Readiness Summary

| Station | Items | Ready | Not Ready |
|---------|-------|-------|-----------|
| 1. Espresso Bar | 12 | | |
| 2. POS / Till | 7 | | |
| 3. Food Display | 7 | | |
| 4. Floor / Customer | 8 | | |
| 5. Support Stock | 4 | | |
| **TOTAL** | **38** | | |

**Rush readiness:**
- 36-38 Ready: Fully prepared. Bring it on.
- 32-35 Ready: Minor gaps -- resolve before doors open.
- Below 32: Not ready. Delay opening if necessary. Fix critical items first (milk, machine, POS).

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Shift Lead: _______________
- Time verified: _______________
- Doors open at: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Grinder Not Dialled In

**Why it happens:** Opening barista puts beans in the hopper and pulls the first customer shot without checking. Coffee has changed overnight (humidity, age, temperature). First 5-10 drinks are sour or bitter.
**How to prevent:** Dial-in is item 1.2 -- it happens before any customer drink. Pull 2-3 test shots, adjust, confirm recipe. Record in T087-04.
**If it is already happening:** Stop serving. Pull test shots now. Adjust grind. The 60-second delay is worth avoiding 10 remakes.

### Risk 2: Running Out of Milk at 08:30

**Why it happens:** Morning barista stocks 4L of milk. Morning rush uses 12L. By 08:30, someone is running to the back fridge mid-queue.
**How to prevent:** Stock for peak, not average. Check item 1.5 quantities against your typical morning usage. If you go through 15L by 10:00, start with 8L at the station and 8L in backup.
**If it is already happening:** Brief the team: "We stock for the rush, not the first hour." Adjust par levels using T087-02.

### Risk 3: Food Display Empty During Peak

**Why it happens:** Pastry delivery arrives at 07:00 but nobody unpacks and displays them until 08:00. Customer walks in at 07:45, sees an empty case, and walks out.
**How to prevent:** Food display (items 3.1-3.7) must be complete by 07:15 -- before doors open. If the delivery is late, display whatever is available and add items as they arrive.
**If it is already happening:** Assign food display as a specific opening task to a named person. It is not "whoever gets to it."

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add filter coffee / batch brew prep (grind, brew, set timer for refresh). Add pour-over station setup if applicable.
**Chain / Franchise:** Align station names and item lists with your brand opening standards. Add any brand-required checks.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify to Stations 1, 2, and 5. Add vehicle/stall-specific items (generator, water tank level, waste container).
**Bakery Cafe:** Expand Station 3 significantly -- oven preheat, proving status, morning bake schedule, display rotation plan.

**Making it yours:**
- Laminate and mount at the pass or POS -- visible to the whole opening team
- Adjust quantities (milk, cups, beans) based on your actual daily usage
- Add a "rush debrief" at 10:30: "What did we run out of? What was not ready?"
- Feed recurring NOT READY items into T087-02 (Station Par Level Guide) for permanent fixes

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T087-02 -- Station Par Level Guide (L2, template)
- T087-03 -- Visual Station Setup Guide (L2, SOP)
- T087-04 -- Espresso Dial-In Record (L2, tracker)
- T087-05 -- Prep Completion Time Log (L3, tracker)

**Related blocks:**
- B086

**Parent block:** B087 -- Pre-Rush Mise-en-Place
**Module:** M05: Service Flow & Peak Management
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
